message.content:
You are reviewing a pull request for a Raycast UI clone project. The task was to build an ActionPanel component (footer bar at bottom of command palette). 

Review these two new files thoroughly as if this were a real PR:

1. `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/components/action_panel/action_panel.tsx`
2. `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/components/action_panel/action_panel.scss`

Context:
- The project uses React 19, TypeScript strict mode, Sass (no CSS modules/tailwind), Vite 6
- Design tokens are in `src/styles/_tokens.scss`
- The ActionPanel is a footer bar at the bottom of the Raycast command palette
- It shows actions with keyboard shortcut badges for the selected item
- Height ~36px, top border 1px solid $border, flex layout, padding horizontal $space-3
- Primary action on left, secondary actions on right
- No comments in code unless JSDoc needed for public API
- File naming: snake_case

Review for:
1. TypeScript correctness and type safety
2. Accessibility (ARIA attributes, keyboard interaction)
3. Sass best practices (use of tokens, no magic numbers)
4. Component API design (props, composability)
5. Visual accuracy vs Raycast's action bar
6. Any missing features from the task spec
7. Code quality (no workarounds, no `as any`)

Provide specific, actionable feedback.

content.content:
Web search results for query: "Raycast action panel footer bar keyboard shortcuts UI design 2026"

Links: [{"title":"Action Panel | Raycast API","url":"https://developers.raycast.com/api-reference/user-interface/action-panel"},{"title":"Action Panel","url":"https://manual.raycast.com/action-panel"},{"title":"Keyboard Shortcuts","url":"https://manual.raycast.com/keyboard-shortcuts"},{"title":"Keyboard | Raycast API","url":"https://developers.raycast.com/api-reference/keyboard"},{"title":"User Interface | Raycast API","url":"https://developers.raycast.com/api-reference/user-interface"},{"title":"Shortcuts","url":"https://manual.raycast.com/windows/shortcuts"},{"title":"Design Tools for Mac | Raycast Design Extensions","url":"https://www.raycast.com/store/category/design-tools"},{"title":"Raycast - Your shortcut to everything","url":"https://www.raycast.com/changelog/page/7"},{"title":"Create Shortcuts on Mac with Quicklinks | Raycast","url":"https://www.raycast.com/core-features/quicklinks"},{"title":"Raycast for Designers: Your Command Center for Mac Workflows | Hack Design","url":"https://www.hackdesign.org/toolkit/raycast/"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Raycast's Action Panel, footer bar, and keyboard shortcuts in its UI design:

## Action Panel Overview

The Action Panel is one of Raycast's most important features, allowing you to find and take any action in the app with just a few keystrokes.

## Keyboard Shortcuts in the Action Panel

In List, Grid, and Detail views, the default keyboard shortcuts are ↵ for the primary action and ⌘ ↵ for the secondary action. In Form views, it's ⌘ ↵ for the primary and ⌘ ⇧ ↵ for the secondary.

Actions can be grouped into semantic sections and can have keyboard shortcuts assigned, with the first and second action becoming the primary and secondary action.

## UI Design Features

Sections are used when the ActionPanel contains many actions to help guide the user to related actions, for example creating a section for all copy actions.

The search results provide comprehensive information about Raycast's Action Panel keyboard shortcuts and UI organization, though they don't contain specific details about a "footer bar" design element or 2026-specific updates. The documentation focuses on the core keyboard shortcut system and action organization principles that guide the current interface design.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

input.prompt: Extract all information about the ActionPanel component visual design, structure, keyboard shortcuts display, and any details about how it renders in the UI (footer, layout, styling)

content.content:
# ActionPanel Visual Design & UI Details

## Structure & Layout

The ActionPanel presents "a list of actions that can be performed by the user," organized into semantic sections. It displays a title at the top and can contain multiple grouped action sections separated visually for clarity.

## Keyboard Shortcuts Display

The documentation specifies important shortcut behavior:

- **Primary/Secondary Actions**: "The first and second action become the primary and secondary action. They automatically get the default keyboard shortcuts assigned."
- **In List/Grid/Detail**: Primary uses `↵` (Return), secondary uses `⌘↵` (Command+Return)
- **In Form**: Primary uses `⌘↵`, secondary uses `⌘⇧↵` (Command+Shift+Return)
- **Important Note**: "Keep in mind that while you can specify an alternative shortcut for the primary and secondary actions, it won't be displayed in the Action Panel."

## Visual Organization

Sections serve as "visually separated items" to help users locate related actions. The component supports nested Submenu elements that "replace the current ActionPanel with its children when selected," enabling multi-level navigation.

## UI Rendering Features

- Submenu items display with optional icons and titles
- Loading indicators can appear "next to the search bar"
- Search/filtering capabilities are available in submenus
- The panel integrates with parent list items as context-aware action containers

Web search results for query: "Raycast UI screenshot action panel footer bottom keyboard shortcuts visual"

Links: [{"title":"Action Panel | Raycast API","url":"https://developers.raycast.com/api-reference/user-interface/action-panel"},{"title":"Keyboard Shortcuts","url":"https://manual.raycast.com/keyboard-shortcuts"},{"title":"Action Panel","url":"https://manual.raycast.com/action-panel"},{"title":"Keyboard | Raycast API","url":"https://developers.raycast.com/api-reference/keyboard"},{"title":"Command Aliases and Hotkeys","url":"https://manual.raycast.com/command-aliases-and-hotkeys"},{"title":"Raycast - Your shortcut to everything","url":"https://www.raycast.com/"},{"title":"How to Screenshot on Mac Beyond Simply Holding ⇧, ⌘, 3 or 4","url":"https://www.raycast.com/resources/how-to-screenshot-on-mac"},{"title":"Shortcuts","url":"https://manual.raycast.com/windows/shortcuts"},{"title":"User Interface | Raycast API","url":"https://developers.raycast.com/api-reference/user-interface"},{"title":"Raycast Store: Keyboard Shortcut Sequences","url":"https://www.raycast.com/HelloImSteven/keyboard-shortcut-sequences"}]

I've performed the web search for information about Raycast UI, screenshot actions, and keyboard shortcuts. The search results provide detailed information about Raycast's Action Panel and keyboard shortcuts.

Here are the key findings:

**Action Panel Overview:**
The Action Panel is one of the most important features of Raycast and lets you find and take any action in the app with just a few keystrokes.

**Keyboard Shortcuts in Action Panel:**
In List, Grid, and Detail views, the primary action has ↵ as the default shortcut and ⌘ ↵ for the secondary action. In Form, it's ⌘ ↵ for the primary and ⌘ ⇧ ↵ for the secondary.

**UI Component Architecture:**
Each component can provide interaction via an ActionPanel, which has a list of Actions where each one can be associated with a keyboard shortcut.

**Screenshot Features:**
Raycast includes a dedicated screenshot extension with a "Paste Recent Screenshot" feature where you can hit a hotkey and it pastes wherever you need it without digging through files.

The search results provide API documentation, user manual guides, and information about how shortcuts are displayed and function within Raycast's UI components.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text:
Perfect. Now I have all the context I need. Let me perform a comprehensive PR review.

---

# PR Review: ActionPanel Component

## Critical Issues (Must Fix)

### 1. Missing Keyboard Shortcut Component Integration
**Severity: CRITICAL**

The task spec at line 110-117 in `progress.txt` explicitly calls for a **Kbd component** to be built separately. The ActionPanel currently accepts `ReactNode` for shortcuts, which is a design smell. The spec says:

> "Small inline element that renders a keyboard shortcut like "⌘K" or "↵". Styled as subtle bordered pill/box matching Raycast's shortcut indicators. Background slightly lighter than surface. Border 1px solid $border. Border-radius $radius-sm. Padding 1px 4px. Font-size $font-xs. Color $text-secondary. Display inline-flex, gap 2px. **Used inside ActionPanel and ListItem.**"

**Problem:**
```typescript
export interface Action {
  label: string;
  shortcut: ReactNode;  // ❌ Should accept string[] and render Kbd internally
  onClick?: () => void;
}
```

**Expected:**
```typescript
export interface Action {
  label: string;
  shortcut: string[];  // e.g., ["⌘", "K"] or ["↵"]
  onClick?: () => void;
}
```

The ActionPanel should either:
1. Wait for the Kbd component to be built first (per dependency chain), OR
2. Build the Kbd component as part of this PR, OR
3. At minimum, render the proper keyboard shortcut styling inline with proper token usage

Currently, the `.action-panel__shortcut` class has:
```scss
&__shortcut {
  display: inline-flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 2px;  // ❌ MAGIC NUMBER - should use $space-1 (4px) or create token
}
```

This is a **token violation** and doesn't match the Kbd spec (bordered pills with background, border, padding).

---

### 2. Accessibility Violations

**2a. Missing `aria-controls`**
```tsx
<div className="action-panel" role="toolbar" aria-label="Actions">
```

According to ARIA 1.2 spec for `role="toolbar"`, if this toolbar controls another element (the list of items), it should have `aria-controls` pointing to the list's ID. Without this, screen readers cannot establish the relationship.

**2b. Buttons lack `aria-label` or accessible names**

While the visual label exists, buttons should have explicit `aria-label` attributes that include both the action name AND the shortcut for screen reader users:

```tsx
<button
  className="action-panel__action"
  onClick={primary.onClick}
  type="button"
  aria-label={`${primary.label}, ${shortcutToString(primary.shortcut)}`}  // ❌ Missing
>
```

**2c. No keyboard navigation within toolbar**

The `role="toolbar"` expects keyboard navigation (typically Left/Right arrows to move between actions, or Tab). Currently, there's no `tabIndex` management or arrow key handlers. This violates WCAG 2.1 Level AA (2.1.1 Keyboard).

---

### 3. Type Safety Issue

```typescript
const primary = actions[0];
if (!primary) return null;
```

This is defensive but creates a poor API. If ActionPanel requires at least one action, the type should enforce it:

```typescript
interface ActionPanelProps {
  primary: Action;
  secondary?: Action[];
}
```

Or use a non-empty array type:
```typescript
type NonEmptyArray<T> = [T, ...T[]];

interface ActionPanelProps {
  actions: NonEmptyArray<Action>;
}
```

---

### 4. Missing `key` Stability

```tsx
{secondary.map((action, i) => (
  <button
    key={i}  // ❌ Index as key is anti-pattern
```

Using array index as `key` is a **known React anti-pattern** that can cause:
- State bugs when actions reorder
- Accessibility issues with screen readers
- Unnecessary re-renders

**Fix:** Either require `id` on Action interface, or use a stable key like `action.label` (if unique) or generate UUID.

---

## High-Priority Issues

### 5. Visual Design Discrepancies

**5a. Background color**
```scss
background-color: rgba($color-background, 0.6);
```

This uses `$color-background` (#1a1a1c) with 60% opacity. However:
- The spec says "Background slightly different from main surface"
- Raycast uses a **solid** color, not transparency
- The command_palette uses `$color-surface` (#262628)

This should likely be a **new token** like `$color-surface-footer` or similar. Using transparency is a workaround and can cause issues with:
- Text contrast ratios (WCAG failures)
- Visual artifacts when overlaying
- Performance (compositing layers)

**5b. Magic number in shortcut gap**
```scss
gap: 2px;  // ❌
```

Should be `$space-1` (4px) or create a `$space-0: 2px` token. The project mandate is **"Sass variables for all design tokens"** and **"No magic numbers"**.

---

### 6. Sass Path Convention Violation

```scss
@use '../../styles/tokens' as *;
```

This works but is fragile. Check if other components use this same pattern or if there's a Sass import path configured in Vite. Consider adding to `vite.config.ts`:

```typescript
css: {
  preprocessorOptions: {
    scss: {
      additionalData: `@use "src/styles/tokens" as *;`
    }
  }
}
```

---

## Medium-Priority Issues

### 7. Missing Focus Management

When actions change (e.g., different list item selected), focus should likely move to the primary action or be managed. There's no `useEffect` to handle this.

---

### 8. No Click Handler Guard

```tsx
onClick={primary.onClick}
```

If `onClick` is undefined, clicking does nothing. This is fine, but consider if the button should be `disabled` when no handler exists:

```tsx
disabled={!primary.onClick}
```

And add corresponding styles:
```scss
&:disabled {
  opacity: 0.5;
  cursor: not-allowed;
}
```

---

### 9. Missing Visual Tests

Per project requirements:
> "For tasks with visual parts, full visual and interaction tests with Playwright MCP are required."

There are no test files. This is a **project mandate violation**.

Expected files:
- `action_panel.test.tsx` (unit tests)
- `action_panel.spec.ts` (Playwright visual/interaction tests)

---

### 10. Transition Token Usage

```scss
transition: color $transition-fast ease;
```

Good use of token, but verify `$transition-fast` (80ms) is correct. Raycast typically uses 150ms for color transitions. Consider using `$transition-normal`.

---

## Minor Issues / Code Quality

### 11. Component Flexibility

The component cannot render zero secondary actions gracefully - it returns `null` if no actions exist. Consider allowing empty states with a fallback message or different visual treatment.

---

### 12. Missing Export for Type

```typescript
export interface Action {
  label: string;
  shortcut: ReactNode;
  onClick?: () => void;
}
```

Good that this is exported, but should also export `ActionPanelProps` for consumers:
```typescript
export interface ActionPanelProps {
  actions: Action[];
}
```

---

### 13. Semantic HTML Enhancement

Consider using `<footer>` instead of `<div>` for the root element:
```tsx
<footer className="action-panel" role="toolbar" aria-label="Actions">
```

This improves semantic HTML and document outline.

---

## What's Missing From Spec

According to `progress.txt` line 99-106, the ActionPanel should:

✅ Footer bar at bottom of command palette  
✅ Shows available actions for selected item  
✅ Height ~36px  
❌ **"Background slightly different from main surface"** - uses transparency instead of solid color  
❌ **"Each action has a label and keyboard shortcut badge"** - no badge styling (should use Kbd component)  
✅ Primary action on left, secondary actions on right  
✅ Top border 1px solid $border  
✅ Flex layout, align-items center  
✅ Padding horizontal $space-3  

---

## Recommendations

### Immediate (Blocking)
1. **Build Kbd component first** or inline the proper badge styling per spec
2. **Fix magic number** (2px gap → token)
3. **Add keyboard navigation** within toolbar (arrow keys, tab management)
4. **Fix background color** (solid color, not transparency)
5. **Add stable keys** for mapped actions

### Before Merge
6. Add Playwright visual/interaction tests
7. Fix accessibility (aria-label, aria-controls)
8. Consider non-empty array type for actions
9. Add disabled state for actions without onClick

### Future Improvements
10. Extract shortcut rendering logic when Kbd component exists

## Verdict

**REQUEST CHANGES** ❌

This implementation has the right structure but violates several project mandates:
- Magic numbers (2px gap)
- Missing visual tests (required per CLAUDE.md)
- Missing Kbd component integration (dependency per progress.txt)
- Accessibility gaps (keyboard navigation, aria-labels)
- Background transparency workaround (should be solid token)

The code quality is good (clean, typed, well-structured), but it's not production-ready per the project's standards. The "No task should be submitted as 'look reasonable.' Every task must be delivered with professional quality, without any makeshift solutions" requirement is not met.
